defects: Hand size, fist silhouette, and wrist attachment visibly morph between frames.
Boot and foot length, orientation, and ankle attachment change inconsistently across the walk.
Knee, ankle, wrist, and elbow relationships do not form one coherent articulated body over time.
Forearms, fists, lower legs, and boots change apparent scale between adjacent frames.
Boots and clothing edges deform at their limb attachment points instead of staying fitted to the same character.
The six frames read as separately redrawn limb configurations rather than a continuous SE walk cycle.
